name: Whitsbury
overview: Whitsbury is a village and civil parish on the Hampshire/Wiltshire/Dorset border, close to Fordingbridge (about 2.5 miles) and on the edge of the Cranborne Chase National Landscape; it was part of Wiltshire until transferred to Hampshire in 1895 and had a population of around 210 in 2011. The village is a straggling north-south street of thatched and timbered houses, overlooked by the Iron Age hillfort of Whitsbury Castle Ditches. It is best known for its racehorse-training heritage: the Whitsbury gallops and Whitsbury Manor Stables have trained horses since the 19th century, most famously the great Desert Orchid.
character: A picturesque, peaceful downland village with strong equestrian and racing roots, period cottages and far-reaching countryside and Dark Skies views.
amenities: Everyday shops, schools and services about 2.5 miles away in Fordingbridge

schools.name: The Burgate School and Sixth Form

commutes.notes: About 8 miles north via the A338
Around 2.5 miles for everyday shops and services
From Salisbury station; no railway station locally

prices.summary: A small, sought-after village of thatched and period cottages; recent two- and three-bedroom cottage asking prices have been around 600,000-650,000 pounds, with council tax on individual cottages seen at Band F. Use the SP6 sold-price record for fuller context.

thingsToDo: Walk up to Whitsbury Castle Ditches Iron Age hillfort
Walking and riding on Whitsbury Down and the racing gallops
Cranborne Chase Dark Skies Reserve stargazing
Rockbourne Roman Villa nearby; the New Forest National Park a few miles east
pros: Retains a village pub and church with a strong community
Striking downland setting in the Cranborne Chase National Landscape and Dark Skies Reserve
Famous equestrian/racing heritage and excellent walking and riding
Within reach of Fordingbridge, Salisbury and the New Forest
cons: No nearby railway station - nearest is Salisbury (~8 miles)
Car-dependent; limited and relatively pricey housing stock
whoItSuits: Buyers drawn to a picturesque downland village with a pub, racing heritage and superb countryside, within easy reach of Fordingbridge and Salisbury.

sources.label: Whitsbury - Mapcarta (St Leonard's Church, The Cartwheel)
Whitsbury Manor Stables - Marcus Tregoning Racing
